message:
test(poll): cover tracked-region EFAULT guards in poll_abi

Companion to the poll_abi tracked-region guard commit. Each new
case allocates a tracked buffer one byte short of the required
struct size via the malloc_abi tracker, asserts the tracker reports
remaining < required (so the test fails loudly if the tracker
regresses to "untracked"), then proves the corresponding poll_abi
entry point rejects with -1 + EFAULT before issuing the syscall.

New helpers (test-private):

* tracked_zeroed_bytes(len) / free_tracked — round-trip a malloc
  allocation through the public ABI so the tracker observes it the
  same way production callers would.
* assert_known_short(raw, required) — uses the
  malloc_known_remaining_for_tests window into the malloc_abi
  tracker; produces a diagnostic message on regression.
* clear_errno / errno_value — read/write the tracked errno cell
  via __errno_location().

New test cases:

* poll_rejects_tracked_short_pollfd_array — tracked array shorter
  than size_of::<pollfd>().
* ppoll_rejects_tracked_short_pollfd_array — same as poll, with
  null sigmask + zero timeout to exercise the optional-pointer
  branch.
* select_rejects_tracked_short_fd_set — tracked fd_set shorter
  than the dynamic size_of::<c_long>() needed for nfds=1.
* epoll_ctl_add_rejects_tracked_short_event — exercises the ADD/
  MOD-only branch in epoll_ctl with a short event struct on a
  real epfd + pipe pair (cleaned up in scope).
* epoll_wait_rejects_tracked_short_events — short events array
  with maxevents=1.

Each case round-trips through the live malloc_abi tracker so a
regression in fallback_insert_sized, free, or
known_remaining surfaces here in addition to the existing
unistd_abi test coverage.
